perturbation.original_text: 1.1 Net Investment Income. "Fund Investment Income", as defined in paragraph 1.2 below, minus "Fund Operating Expenses" as defined in paragraph 1.3 below equals Net Investment Income. If the Fund's Net Investment Income on any day is below zero, the Investment Adviser shall waive its Advisory Fee or reimburse the Fund an amount (defined as "Expense Waiver") sufficient to produce a Net Investment Income of zero.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 Net Investment Income. "Fund Investment Income", as defined in paragraph 1.2 below, minus "Fund Operating Expenses" as defined in paragraph 1.3 below equals Net Investment Income. If the Fund's Net Investment Income on any day is below zero, the Investment Adviser may waive its Advisory Fee or reimburse the Fund an amount (defined as "Expense Waiver") to produce a positive Net Investment Income.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall waive' to 'may waive', the contract creates ambiguity regarding the Investment Adviser's responsibility to cover the Net Investment Income, contradicting the statement that the purpose of the agreement is to 'maintain the Fund's daily “Net Investment Income” (as defined in paragraph 1.1 below) at or in excess of zero.' This creates uncertainty about the primary objective of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Trust and the Investment Adviser have determined that it is appropriate and in the best interests of the Fund and its shareholders to maintain the Fund's daily "Net Investment Income" (as defined in paragraph 1.1 below) at or in excess of zero.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall waive' to 'may waive' introduces a direct contradiction. The original text mandated the Investment Adviser to cover the deficit to maintain a zero or positive Net Investment Income. The changed text makes this optional, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, which states the agreement's purpose is to 'maintain the Fund's daily “Net Investment Income”... at or in excess of zero.' This creates uncertainty about the Investment Adviser's obligation, undermining the agreement's stated objective and creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Right of Recovery. The Investment Adviser shall have the option to recover the full amount waived, paid or reimbursed (the Expense Waiver) by the Investment Adviser on any day on which the Fund's Net Investment Income exceeds zero. In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day, in which case such portion of the Expense Waiver elected by the Investment Adviser for recovery that day shall then be due and payable. If, and to the extent, the payment by the Fund to the Investment Adviser of the Expense Waiver would cause the Fund's Net Investment Income to fall below zero, such payment shall be deferred until such date on which the
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Right of Recovery. The Investment Adviser shall have the option to recover a portion of the amount waived, paid or reimbursed (the Expense Waiver) by the Investment Adviser on any day on which the Fund's Net Investment Income exceeds zero. In no event, however, shall the Expense Waiver, or any portion thereof, constitute an obligation of the Fund to the Investment Adviser on any such day unless the Investment Adviser has expressly exercised its right to recover all or a portion of the Expense Waiver on that day, in which case such portion of the Expense Waiver elected by the Investment Adviser for recovery that day shall then be due and payable. If, and to the extent, the payment by the Fund to the Investment Adviser of the Expense Waiver would cause the Fund's Net Investment Income to fall below zero, such payment shall be deferred until such date on which the
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'full amount waived' to 'a portion of the amount waived', this creates a contradiction because the definition of 'Expense Waiver' is not clarified. The absence of a standard on how much will be waived, creates incertainty. Since Section 1.1 mentions that the Expense Waiver should be sufficient to make the Net Investment Income zero, the partial waiver creates an in-text contradiction, making that objective uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Net Investment Income. "Fund Investment Income", as defined in paragraph 1.2 below, minus "Fund Operating Expenses" as defined in paragraph 1.3 below equals Net Investment Income. If the Fund's Net Investment Income on any day is below zero, the Investment Adviser may waive its Advisory Fee or reimburse the Fund an amount (defined as "Expense Waiver") to produce a positive Net Investment Income.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the 'full amount' waived be recoverable, supporting Section 1.1's intention to bring Net Investment Income to zero. By changing this to 'a portion', the modified text contradicts the purpose outlined in Section 1.1. Since the expense waiver might not be sufficient to bring the Net Investment Income to zero, this creates an inconsistency, because it's no longer guaranteed that the fund will have a positive Net Investment Income. Thus the change directly undermines the intention of Section 1.1.

perturbation.original_text: In connection with the obligation of the Investment Adviser to maintain the Fund's Net Investment Income as set forth in section 1 herein, the Underwriter may waive its Rule 12b-1 fees contemplated by the Underwriting Agreement, and shall have the same rights to be reimbursed by the Fund as the Investment Adviser, as set forth in Section 1 herein, to the extent the Investment Adviser has not already been reimbursed by the Fund for its payment of Rule 12b-1 fees.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with the objective of the Investment Adviser to strive for a positive Fund's Net Investment Income as set forth in section 1 herein, the Underwriter may waive its Rule 12b-1 fees contemplated by the Underwriting Agreement, and shall have the same rights to be reimbursed by the Fund as the Investment Adviser, as set forth in Section 1 herein, to the extent the Investment Adviser has not already been reimbursed by the Fund for its payment of Rule 12b-1 fees.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'obligation to maintain' to 'objective to strive for', the perturbation weakens the Underwriter's responsibility to waive its Rule 12b-1 fees to contribute towards maintaining a positive net investment income. Given that in Section 1.1 the 'shall waive' to 'may waive' has been changed, and here also there is a weakening of the statement. This can mean that the Underwriter may choose not to contribute to the Net Investment Income. This conflicts with the stated purpose of the agreement to maintain a positive Net Investment Income.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Trust and the Investment Adviser have determined that it is appropriate and in the best interests of the Fund and its shareholders to maintain the Fund's daily "Net Investment Income" (as defined in paragraph 1.1 below) at or in excess of zero.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Investment Adviser's 'obligation to maintain' the Fund's Net Investment Income, while the changed text weakens this to an 'objective to strive for'. This change creates a contradiction with the 'WHEREAS' clause, which states the Fund's intention to 'maintain' the Net Investment Income at or above zero. The original text aligns with the intention to maintain, but the change suggests a less firm commitment, creating a strong and direct conflict between the 'objective' and the stated purpose of 'maintain[ing]' the Net Investment Income. This falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
